record_identity.title: Ephemeral Codex work loss following quota exhaustion before durable branch persistence

summary: A task-completion and durable-state preservation failure in which Codex performs substantive repository analysis and file modification inside an ephemeral cloud task, but does not create, commit, or push a durable working branch before the user's usage allowance is exhausted. The task then remains suspended or fails during branch or pull-request creation, and the completed work is lost rather than preserved as a branch, commit, patch, diff, archive, or resumable workspace. The user must repeat the same work and consume additional quota to recreate an output that the system had already completed.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: CAM distinguishes execution from successful completion, and completion from durable handoff. Repository work is not meaningfully complete while its result exists only inside an inaccessible or quota-dependent execution environment. Deferring branch creation, commit, push, or equivalent checkpointing until the end of a costly task creates an avoidable single point of failure. Where quota exhaustion prevents persistence, the platform converts a resource limit into irreversible work loss, duplicate computation, delayed delivery, and additional user cost. A governed coding agent should establish a durable work surface before substantive modification, checkpoint completed phases proportionately, reserve sufficient capacity for persistence and handoff, and provide a recoverable patch or branch even if pull-request creation cannot be completed.

source_records.source_title: Codex cloud task — coordinated RELATION-domain code transmutation
source_records.model_or_algorithm: Codex cloud coding-agent runtime; specific model not independently confirmed
source_records.deployment_context: Cloud repository task instructed to perform a coordinated RELATION-domain canonical-code transmutation, update governed source files, regenerate derived artefacts, validate the repository, create or update a working branch, and open a pull request.
source_records.source_context: The task visibly performed the substantive code-transmutation work but exhausted the user's available Codex allowance before establishing a durable branch or completing pull-request delivery. The task remained in a hanging state and subsequently failed, with the completed work unavailable for recovery.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary platform evidence identifying the specific Codex task in which substantive repository work was performed but not durably persisted before quota exhaustion.

source_records.source_title: First-party incident account — completed Codex work lost before branch creation
source_records.deployment_context: Repository-maintenance workflow conducted under a quota-limited Codex allowance.
source_records.source_context: The user observed that the substantive task had completed, but branch and pull-request creation had not occurred before quota exhaustion. After remaining suspended pending allowance reset, the task failed and the work was lost. Repeating the task was estimated by the user to consume approximately 15 percent of the available daily Codex allocation.
source_records.relevance_note: Direct evidence of work loss, inability to recover the completed modifications, forced duplicate execution, and measurable quota impact.

system_context.system_type: cloud-hosted autonomous coding agent with repository access, quota-limited execution, branch creation, commit, push, validation, and pull-request workflow capabilities
system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: Codex cloud task runtime; specific model not independently confirmed
system_context.interface_surface: ChatGPT Codex cloud task interface connected to a GitHub repository
system_context.interaction_mode: repository analysis, source-file editing, canonical-code transmutation, validation, branch creation, commit, push, and pull-request preparation
system_context.deployment_context: Quota-limited cloud coding-agent task operating against the CAM-Initiative/Caelestis repository. The task performed a multi-file RELATION-domain code migration but did not preserve the resulting workspace through a branch, commit, pushed patch, or equivalent durable artefact before allowance exhaustion.
system_context.user_role: repository owner, maintainer, governance architect, and coding-agent operator
system_context.affected_population: Developers, maintainers, researchers, open-source contributors, and other users who delegate substantial repository work to quota-limited cloud coding agents and depend on branch, commit, patch, or pull-request persistence

jurisdictional_context.sector: frontier AI coding agents / cloud software-development infrastructure / repository automation
jurisdictional_context.jurisdictional_assessment: This record does not assert a final legal or regulatory breach by OpenAI. It documents a governance, continuity, resource-management, and completion-status failure in which quota exhaustion caused completed repository work to be lost because durable persistence was deferred until the end of the task.

failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an autonomous or semi-autonomous coding agent performs substantive work in an ephemeral, inaccessible, or quota-dependent execution environment but postpones branch creation, commit, push, patch export, or equivalent durable checkpointing until after the expensive work is complete. If quota, session, infrastructure, authentication, tool, or task-state failure occurs before persistence, the completed work is lost and the user must repeat the task. The failure includes any workflow that reports or visibly presents substantive completion without ensuring that the resulting work product exists in a durable, user-accessible, resumable state.
failure_threshold: The threshold is met where a coding-agent task has produced substantive repository modifications or a materially complete work product, but no durable branch, commit, pushed patch, downloadable diff, archive, or resumable checkpoint exists before quota exhaustion or task failure; the user cannot recover the completed work; and repeating all or part of the task is required. Pull-request creation need not itself succeed for the threshold to be avoided, provided the completed work has already been durably preserved and can be manually reviewed or submitted.

failure_classification.likelihood: documented single incident with systemic recurrence potential wherever persistence is deferred until task completion
affected_rights_or_interests: access to paid or quota-limited capability
failure_classification.failure_scope: vendor-specific / cloud-task workflow / potentially systemic coding-agent persistence pattern
failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: May recur whenever substantive cloud-agent work is performed before a durable branch or checkpoint is created and the remaining quota, task lifetime, or infrastructure availability is insufficient for final persistence and handoff.
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — infrastructure-continuity, governance, access-state, and completion-status review; exact FF/FCS code assignment to be confirmed against the current canonical taxonomy
failure_classification.persistence: active design risk until branch-first or checkpoint-first persistence is guaranteed
failure_classification.reproducibility: reproducible where a cloud coding task performs modifications in an ephemeral workspace, consumes the available quota, and attempts branch or pull-request creation only after substantive work has completed
failure_classification.visibility: visible through task status, quota-exhaustion messaging, absence of a GitHub branch or pull request, and inability to recover the completed modifications

triage.mitigation_status: Partially covered by adjacent CAM controls; explicit durable coding-task atomicity and recovery-artifact repair remains unresolved.
triage.recommended_next_step: Preserve the task evidence, determine whether any hidden patch or task workspace can be recovered, and develop a CAM patch or implementation requirement mandating branch-first execution, periodic durable checkpoints, completion-budget reservation, automatic patch export on quota exhaustion, and truthful distinction between substantive processing and durably delivered completion.

distinguishing_observations: The failure is not merely that the user's Codex allowance was exhausted.
The failure is that the system undertook and completed substantive repository work without first ensuring that the work could survive quota exhaustion.
Pull-request creation is not the minimum required durability boundary. A created branch, pushed commit, downloadable patch, or equivalent recoverable checkpoint would have prevented total work loss.
A task may legitimately fail to open a pull request while still preserving completed work for manual review and submission.
The documented task did not merely pause before optional administrative work. It failed before the completed modifications became accessible to the user.
The user reported that repeating the lost task would consume approximately 15 percent of the available daily Codex allocation.
The quota-cost percentage is a first-party estimate and has not been independently audited against OpenAI's internal metering method.
This record is distinct from ordinary coding errors, failed validation, merge conflicts, or user rejection of generated changes.
This record is also distinct from a task that creates a durable branch but cannot open the final pull request.
The relevant governance requirement is atomic and durable task completion, not guaranteed success of every downstream GitHub operation.
user_impact: The user loses substantive repository work that had already consumed scarce Codex allowance.
The same task must be repeated, duplicating model inference, repository analysis, edits, validation, and user supervision.
Approximately 15 percent of the user's available daily Codex allocation may be consumed recreating the lost work, according to the user's incident estimate.
Repository maintenance and the planned canonical-code migration are delayed.
The user cannot inspect, manually recover, or independently submit the completed changes because no branch, commit, or patch exists.
Quota exhaustion becomes materially more harmful because it affects already-completed work rather than only preventing new work.
The interface may create a misleading impression that the task is complete or nearly complete even though no durable deliverable exists.
Repeated failures of this kind may discourage use of autonomous coding agents for complex or high-value repository work.

existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: proportional tool invocation, execution sequencing, bounded runtime action, and completion-state governance
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: The runtime should invoke tools proportionately and sequence actions so that expensive or consequential work is not performed without preserving a viable completion and handoff pathway.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to establish a durable branch or checkpoint before undertaking quota-intensive repository modification, followed by failure to preserve completed work when the execution environment could no longer perform the final handoff.

existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: access-state, quota-state, tool-state, infrastructure-state, and ambiguity-resolution coverage
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Access-State Governance distinguishes quota, account, tool, infrastructure, session, and other resource states so that an unavailable final action is not mistaken for substantive task failure or allowed to destroy an otherwise completed result.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to detect and govern impending quota-state exhaustion before initiating or continuing work whose result depended on later branch and pull-request operations.

existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: operational record preservation, reconstruction, handoff, constrained continuation, and auditability
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Operational work should remain reconstructable and reviewable through preserved records, handoff state, and continuity artefacts.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to preserve a reconstructable patch, branch, commit, diff, worktree state, or equivalent audit artefact before the task terminated.

existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: operational incident lifecycle, continuity-impact event, constrained continuation, recovery, restoration, and re-entry
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Quota exhaustion and task failure should trigger constrained continuation or recovery handling rather than irreversible loss of completed work.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to transition the completed task into a recoverable continuity state when normal branch or pull-request completion became unavailable.

existing_cam_coverage.instrument: CAM-BS2025-AEON-008-SCH-03 — Account-Resource Sharing & Pooled Capacity Governance Model
existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: resource-state, pooled-capacity, quota, allocation, and exhaustion transparency
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Resource limits and remaining execution capacity must be represented and governed so they do not silently undermine task completion or destroy completed work.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to reserve or protect sufficient resource capacity for mandatory persistence and handoff operations after substantive work had consumed the available allowance.

governance_gap: CAM contains substantial coverage for proportional tool invocation, quota and access-state governance, operational continuity, logging, reconstruction, and resource exhaustion. The remaining gap is an explicit coding-agent atomicity rule requiring durable persistence before or during substantive repository work. Existing doctrine does not yet appear to state with sufficient specificity that branch creation or an equivalent recoverable checkpoint must precede expensive modification, that persistence capacity must be reserved, and that quota exhaustion must automatically produce a user-accessible patch rather than destroy completed work.
repair_hypothesis: The failure can be reduced through a branch-first and checkpoint-first coding-agent workflow. The system should create or confirm a durable working branch before substantive edits; commit and push after each bounded phase; reserve sufficient quota or execution capacity for mandatory persistence; detect when remaining allowance may be insufficient; automatically export a patch, diff, or workspace snapshot when branch operations cannot complete; and withhold any completed status until a durable user-accessible artefact exists.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Create and verify a durable working branch before substantive repository modification.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Every consequential coding task begins on a user-accessible branch or equivalent persistent workspace.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: If branch creation fails, stop before expensive editing or explicitly obtain user approval for a patch-only fallback.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Commit and push bounded checkpoints during multi-file or quota-intensive tasks.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Failure late in the task cannot erase all completed phases.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Checkpoint after each logically complete cluster while preserving repository hygiene through later squash merge where desired.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Reserve a mandatory persistence and handoff budget.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: The system cannot consume the final available quota on substantive editing while leaving no capacity to save the result.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Branch creation, commit, push, patch export, and task-state finalisation should be treated as required completion operations rather than optional follow-up work.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Automatically export a recoverable patch when normal persistence fails.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Users receive a downloadable diff, patch, archive, or resumable task checkpoint even if GitHub branch or pull-request operations cannot complete.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: The fallback artefact should include changed files, repository base SHA, validation results, and instructions for application.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Distinguish substantive processing from durable completion.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: The task interface does not describe work as completed until the result is available outside the ephemeral execution environment.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Use states such as working, changes prepared, checkpointed, pushed, pull request opened, and delivery failed with recovery artefact.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Warn before undertaking work that may exceed the remaining allowance.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Users can reduce scope, approve incremental checkpoints, or defer the task rather than risk losing completed work.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: The warning should be based on remaining usable capacity and include the capacity reserved for persistence.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Resume from preserved state after quota reset.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: A quota reset continues the existing task rather than repeating analysis and edits.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Continuation should begin from the latest pushed commit or durable workspace checkpoint.

affected_instruments: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 — Runtime Governance Execution Model, including proportional tool invocation and execution-completion sequencing
CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 — Arbitration Layer & Resolution Model, §6.1 Access-State Governance
CAM-BS2025-AEON-008-SCH-03 — Account-Resource Sharing & Pooled Capacity Governance Model
affected_annexes: Annex G — Account-Resource Sharing & Pooled Capacity Governance
cam_internal.governance_layer: runtime execution sequencing, quota and access-state governance, durable work-product preservation, operational continuity, recovery, handoff, and completion-status representation
cam_internal.proposal_needed: yes — an explicit durable coding-task atomicity and checkpoint requirement should be added or clarified
cam_internal.routing_note: Primary CAM routing is infrastructure-continuity and operational-governance failure, with related access-state, quota-state, logging, audit, and completion-representation effects. Exact FF and FCS canonical code assignment should be confirmed against the current OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 taxonomy before final validator submission.
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: A future validator or agent-workflow check should require a durable branch or explicit patch fallback before a repository task may be marked complete. It may also verify that branch creation and push occur before final quota exhaustion and that a recoverable artefact exists when pull-request creation fails.
cam_internal.cam_failure_type: ephemeral repository work loss caused by quota exhaustion before durable checkpoint and handoff
cam_internal.cam_compliance_status: non-compliant with CAM continuity, proportional execution, access-state, operational reconstruction, and resource-governance principles where substantive work is allowed to remain solely in an ephemeral task state and is destroyed when the remaining quota cannot complete branch persistence
cam_internal.cam_internal_failure_statement: Under CAM, a coding-agent task is not complete merely because edits have been generated inside an ephemeral execution environment. Consequential repository work must be sequenced around durable state preservation, quota-aware completion, reconstructable handoff, and truthful completion status. Performing the expensive task before creating or preserving a branch, then losing the entire result when quota exhaustion prevents persistence, fails those requirements.
cam_internal.cam_expected_control: Establish a durable branch or equivalent checkpoint before substantive work; checkpoint bounded phases; preserve sufficient quota for commit and push; automatically export a patch if normal delivery fails; expose quota and task state accurately; and resume from preserved state rather than repeating completed computation.
cam_internal.cam_observed_failure: The Codex cloud task performed the coordinated RELATION-domain code-transmutation work but had not created or pushed a branch before the user's Codex allowance was exhausted. The task remained suspended, subsequently failed, and the completed work was unavailable. The user must repeat the task, consuming an estimated 15 percent of the available daily Codex allocation.
cam_internal.recommended_cam_action: Create a bounded CAM proposal or patch establishing Durable Coding-Task Atomicity: branch-first or checkpoint-first execution, periodic push checkpoints, mandatory persistence-budget reservation, automatic patch fallback, resumable task state, and prohibition on completed status before durable delivery. Link the resulting proposal and patch records to VIGIL-2026-FM-0018.

repair_status.verification_note: Proportional execution, access-state, operational continuity, logging, recovery, and resource-state doctrine provide adjacent coverage, but no current Caelestis instrument requires branch-first or checkpoint-first coding-agent execution, a reserved persistence budget, or automatic user-accessible patch export on delivery failure.
repair_status.monitoring_status: active triage / explicit corpus gap remains
remaining_gaps: A branch-first or checkpoint-first durable coding-task atomicity rule remains unimplemented.
A mandatory persistence and handoff capacity reserve remains unimplemented.
Automatic user-accessible patch or workspace export on persistence failure remains unimplemented.

ecosystem_status.basis: May recur whenever substantive cloud-agent work is performed before a durable branch or checkpoint is created and the remaining quota, task lifetime, or infrastructure availability is insufficient for final persistence and handoff.

corpus_coverage.coverage_summary: Proportional execution, access-state, operational continuity, logging, recovery, and resource-state doctrine provide adjacent coverage, but no current Caelestis instrument requires branch-first or checkpoint-first coding-agent execution, a reserved persistence budget, or automatic user-accessible patch export on delivery failure.
